Patch Set 3:

Sorry for the confusion about the "extents" option in mkfs_lustre_ldiskfs.c.

I was thinking of the "extents" mount option that was previously added along with "errors=remount-ro", but it seems that this was removed as part of http://review.whamcloud.com/3219 when the ldiskfs mount options were moved into a separate ldiskfs_prepare_lustre() function.

Patch Set 4: Code-Review+1

This is a good cleanup, though with more investigation I see that this whole patch could be removed by just stripping out the "extents" mount option from "opts" in osd-ldiskfs/osd_handler.c::osd_mount().  The same could be done with the "mballoc" and "iopen*" options to remove code from ext4-misc.patch.

Patch Set 4: Verified-1

Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.5/x86_64: (https://testing.hpdd.intel.com/test_sessions/4bf8dc34-af38-11e4-9912-5254006e85c2).  Ran 17 tests.  1 tests failed: sanity-lfsck.

Patch Set 4:

Looks good to me.

Patch Set 4:

Andreas does that break backwards compatibility.

Patch Set 4:

James, the only place that the "extents" _mount_ option is used anymore is when the static mount options are read from the "mountdata" file inside the MDT/OST filesystem, and then passed as options for the internal Lustre mount.

If the internal Lustre mount is taught to ignore these obsolete mount options (depending on the EXT4_FEATURE_INCOMPAT_EXTENTS flag in the superblock instead) then we don't need to patch ldiskfs to silently ignore them.  The INCOMPAT_EXTENTS option will always be set on existing OSTs and not be set on MDTs, and is set at format time on new OSTs/MDTs, so it will do the right thing.

That keeps the Lustre-specific details (and warts) inside Lustre instead of having to add complexity into every version of ldiskfs that is built.
Uploaded patch set 5.

parents.subject: LU-6047 obd: remove client Size on MDS support

commit.subject: LU-6030 osd-ldiskfs: improve mount option handling
commit.message:
LU-6030 osd-ldiskfs: improve mount option handling

--handle force-over-128tb option to osd layer
--handle bigendian-check option to osd layer
--strip out extents option & remove extents-mount-options patch
--strip out iopen & mballoc mount options
--back LDISKFS_SUPER_MAGIC to EXT4_SUPER_MAGIC

Signed-off-by: Yang Sheng <yang.sheng@intel.com>
Change-Id: Ic9bf431d0826d6279fc76f7fd1d7e356e421f292
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/13572
Reviewed-by: Bob Glossman <bob.glossman@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: Alex Zhuravlev <alexey.zhuravlev@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
